message:
Add write-optimized Bepsilon-tree, LeanStore page cooling, database cracking, and learned index structures

Four new advanced indexing/storage data structures for the btree crate, each
implementing a distinct research approach to optimize different workload patterns:

- be_tree.rs: Write-optimized Bepsilon-tree (section 15.2) with interior-node
  message buffers that batch pending insert/update/delete operations. Buffers
  flush cascadingly when overflowing. The epsilon parameter controls the
  read/write tradeoff, amortizing random I/O into sequential writes.

- cooling.rs: Page cooling/heating state machine (section 15.3) implementing
  the HOT/COOLING/COLD protocol from LeanStore (Leis et al. 2018) for buffer
  pool eviction. Pages transition through access states with root pages pinned
  HOT to avoid evicting critical tree structure.

- cracking.rs: Database cracking / adaptive indexing (section 8.8) implementing
  Idreos et al. 2007 zero-admin indexing where range queries incrementally
  partition column data. After Q queries on N elements, amortized cost drops
  from O(N) to O(N/Q) without any upfront index build.

- learned_index.rs: Learned index structures (section 8.4) with piecewise linear
  approximation inspired by Kraska et al. 2018. The model predicts key positions,
  replacing B-tree traversal with inference + bounded local search for faster
  point lookups on sorted data.

- lib.rs: Wire all four new modules with pub mod declarations and re-export their
  primary types, configs, and metrics snapshot functions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
